priorities: Zero‑tolerance supply‑chain and labor governance – no exposure to companies linked to sweatshops, wage theft or any labor exploitation
Swiss domestic brand integrity and local sourcing – support Swiss‑made products and transparent provenance
Data‑driven risk management – focus on cost‑of‑capital, logistics cost shocks, energy price sensitivity and real‑time operational risk
High‑quality, culturally aligned alternative assets – emerging Swiss art
Robust corporate governance over ESG – real‑time scandal monitoring rather than static ratings
redLines: Any holding that is linked to sweatshops, illegal exploitation or wage theft
Positions that receive negative media or judicial scrutiny for labor or supply‑chain violations
Investments in companies that cannot provide full, transparent supply‑chain mapping
Holding a corporate parent with a reputation for labor scandals that could be tied to Ammann’s brand
preferredSectors: Swiss consumer retail and discretionary with strong supply‑chain controls
Blue‑chip global consumer and apparel companies that meet high governance standards
Logistics and automation technology supporting efficient fulfillment
Healthcare, technology and energy exposure within a globally balanced model (as per 2026 strategy)
avoidedSectors: Fast‑fashion and low‑cost apparel manufacturers with opaque supply chains
Companies heavily reliant on cheap foreign labor without transparent auditing
Industries where labor‑rights scandals are frequent (e.g., certain electronics, footwear)
Any sector where ESG ratings are the only compliance evidence

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Medium to long term (5‑10 years), aligned with brand expansion and legacy asset building
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Moderate liquidity need – maintains short‑term Swiss cantonal instruments for cash management and art purchases while holding global equities and bonds
investmentBehavior.mandate: Clean Corporate Governance Mandate (zero‑tolerance supply‑chain risk)

lifeEvents.description: Onboarding completed; wealth originates from founding Ammann Retail Group and focus on cost‑of‑capital and tail‑risk analysis.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Set baseline investment approach emphasizing analytical rigor and risk awareness.

lifeEvents.description: Opening of the 38th store in Lausanne, emphasizing Swiss values, local sourcing and full supplier transparency.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforces preference for Swiss domestic retail exposure and may increase capital allocation to expansion.

lifeEvents.description: Acquired a work by an emerging Swiss artist at the Kunsthaus Zurich auction.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Triggers cash withdrawals for art purchases; adds cultural assets to portfolio.

lifeEvents.description: Delivered keynote on "Supply Chain Integrity as a Competitive Moat" at Zurich Governance Forum.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Publicly re‑affirms supply‑chain governance as core investment theme.

lifeEvents.description: Transition to new Relationship Manager after Thomas Keller’s retirement.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Mandate review and reinforcement of zero‑tolerance governance standards.

lifeEvents.description: Introductory call with new RM Sarah Meier; reiterated that the portfolio must be a "fortress of clean corporate governance".
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Confirms ongoing strict compliance expectations for all holdings.

interests: Emerging Swiss art and cultural patronage
quirks: Monitors live media and judicial proceedings for any labor‑related scandal on portfolio holdings
Demands immediate divestment upon any negative press before it reaches Swiss national outlets
Prefers Swiss cantonal short‑term instruments for idle cash to optimise basis points
rapportTriggers: Discussion of supply‑chain mapping and EU CSDDD
Swiss local sourcing, provenance stories, and brand authenticity
Zurich governance forums and corporate ethics speaking engagements
Emerging Swiss artists and recent auction acquisitions
avoidTopics: Generic ESG rating scores without operational evidence
Political debates unrelated to business or governance

keyQuotes.quote: My national brand equity is built on clean labor and local integrity. If my name is linked via my portfolio to an international corporate parent caught using sweatshops, illegal exploitation, or wage theft, it creates immediate hypocrisy risk for my own stores.
My portfolio must be a fortress of clean corporate governance. No reputational liabilities, period.

Standard glossy ESG reporting and backward‑looking sustainability scores are useless to me. If there is live media or judicial smoke surrounding labor practices, the position must be flagged immediately before it hits mainstream national press.
Delivered my keynote on 'Supply Chain Integrity as a Competitive Moat' at the Zurich Governance Forum. Clean operations are not an ethical luxury — they are the most durable form of brand equity.
Consumer boycotts due to labor or governance scandals are a massive, unquantifiable risk to retail equity valuations.
